title: Compliance telemetry hook — fab doctor --audit for fab_get_rules call monitoring
description: (OPTIONAL — can skip to ship core Shadow Mirroring faster) Compliance telemetry to monitor whether fab_get_rules was called before file edits. Server records each fab_get_rules call in an intent ledger (or separate audit log) with timestamp + path. CLI adds fab doctor --audit command that reads edit intent entries (from fab_append_intent ledger or separate edit events) and cross-references against fab_get_rules call log — flags edits where no preceding fab_get_rules call exists within a configurable window. Config switch: audit mode = strict (error) / warn / off. Addresses 'perception-phase vacuum' with observability rather than enforcement.

files.change: Create audit log service: records fab_get_rules call events (path, timestamp, client_hash) to .fabric/audit.jsonl; provides readAuditLog() for CLI consumption.

Add --audit flag to fab doctor: reads .fabric/audit.jsonl (fab_get_rules calls) and cross-references against .fabric/ledger.jsonl (intent entries); flags edits without preceding fab_get_rules within configurable time window (default 5 minutes); output: per-file violation list with timestamps; respects fabric-config.ts audit mode (strict/warn/off).
Add audit_mode: z.enum(['strict','warn','off']).default('warn') to fabric config schema. This controls whether fab doctor --audit exits non-zero (strict), warns only (warn), or skips (off).

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts to understand service pattern; read packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts to understand existing doctor command before modifying
Design audit-log.ts: append-only JSONL file at .fabric/audit.jsonl; each entry has {event: 'get_rules', path, timestamp, client_hash?}
Hook audit log write into get-rules service: after successful rule retrieval, append audit entry
Read packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts and add audit_mode enum field
Implement doctor --audit: parse audit.jsonl + ledger.jsonl, match edits without preceding get_rules within time window, format violation report
Run tsc --noEmit from all affected packages

reference.pattern: packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts service pattern; packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts existing command structure
reference.examples: Append-only JSONL pattern from ledger-append.ts; doctor command structure from existing doctor.ts

criteria: fab doctor --audit reads .fabric/audit.jsonl and .fabric/ledger.jsonl and reports violations
Violation defined as: edit intent entry without preceding fab_get_rules call for same path within 5-minute window
fabric-config.ts schema has audit_mode: strict|warn|off with default warn
fab doctor --audit exits non-zero when audit_mode=strict and violations found
audit.jsonl appended with new entry on each successful fab_get_rules call
tsc --noEmit passes for packages/server, packages/cli, packages/shared

rationale.chosen_approach: Separate audit.jsonl log for compliance tracking; doctor command for CLI-friendly violation reporting; config switch for enforcement level
alternatives_considered: Embed in existing ledger.jsonl (mixes intent records with compliance events — harder to query)
Server-side enforcement (block fab_get_rules-less edits) — too strict; breaks workflows during transition
decision_factors: Observability before enforcement — warn mode by default allows gradual adoption
audit.jsonl is queryable separately from semantic ledger
rationale.tradeoffs: Optional feature; implementation adds server write on every fab_get_rules call (minor I/O overhead)

manual_checks: Call fab_get_rules for a file, then run fab doctor --audit — verify no violation reported
Append a mock ledger edit entry without preceding audit entry, run fab doctor --audit — verify violation reported
Set audit_mode=strict in fabric config, run fab doctor --audit with violation — verify non-zero exit code
success_metrics: tsc --noEmit exits 0 for all affected packages
doctor --audit integration test: 0 violations when all edits preceded by fab_get_rules; correct violation count otherwise
unit: doctor.test.ts: violation detection logic unit test

risks.description: Time-window matching between audit.jsonl and ledger.jsonl requires synchronized timestamps; different machines or offline edits may produce false positives
risks.mitigation: Default audit_mode=warn so false positives are informational only; document limitation in tool help text
risks.fallback: Increase default time window or implement session-based correlation instead of time-based
